review_title: Very good, but could do even better.
author: Vincent G.
pros: The simplicity and the premium support. Divi is a verry good tool for web workers. Simple and accesible
cons: SEO optimization, and especially page load speed, is difficult to achieve despite caching plugins or other minification tools. It's challenging to obtain good scores on the Largest Contentful Paint Index.

description: It's been a great journey so far. I've bought a lifetime license years ago and since then I've used it to build website for a lot of clients.
author: Davey S.
pros: With Divi 5, Elegant Themes is back in the game. After some slow years Divi 5 is adding new features, increased speed and even better to bring ideas to life. In addition, the level of support is fantastic. Problems from small to big are solved within a day through the chat with real humans (specifically heroes [sensitive content hidden]).
cons: Modules could be expanded on a bit, so less CSS is needed. It feels some modules are missing settings.

review_title: A Great First Front End Builder, But Slow.
description: My experience was great until I discovered Elementor and made the switch to a faster, and smoother front end builder.
author: David V.
pros: Divi was a great introduction to front-end drag and drop web builders for someone who is new to WordPress. Customer support was not great, their templates are solid though.
cons: Divi is not great for page speed or page load times. It comes with a lot of bloat. If you try another front end builder like Elementor, it's obvious that Divi requires a lot more clicks and navigation when making edits on the front end.

review_title: Divi VIP Support is fast, precise, and reliable!
description: Overall, Divi has been a strong platform for building and managing WordPress sites. It lets me move fast, keep designs consistent, and adjust layouts without rework. When I do hit an issue, VIP Support has been top notch, which turns problems into quick fixes instead of long delays.
author: Timothy C.
pros: I use Divi for WordPress sites where I need speed, flexibility, and full control over layout. The visual builder is easy to work with and really speeds things up. The biggest standout is VIP Support. The support team responds fast, reads the details, and stays on the issue until it is actually resolved. They do not waste your time with generic replies. When something is urgent, they treat it like it matters. That alone makes Divi a strong long-term choice for client work.
cons: Some updates can introduce small quirks, especially when you have custom CSS or third party plugins in the mix. It is rarely a deal breaker, but I always test changes before pushing to production.

description: I’ve used Divi to build websites for years. It’s simple, it has far fewer bugs, and it doesn't suffer from the security vulnerabilities that plague Elementor.
author: Nacho D.
pros: Divi is a great web builder—a truly fantastic one—but their customer support is what truly sets them apart. They are the only ones who actually take the time to log into your website and find solutions for you.
cons: Seriously, I love Divi. I’ve been using it for years, and every version is better than the last. I've recently been working with version 5 (first the alpha and now the beta) and I couldn't be happier. A lot of people joining the web design team come from Elementor, and once they work with Divi, they never want to touch Elementor again.

review_title: Love Divi - Great design and great support.
description: I find Divi very intuitive and you can design lovely sights with it. Also, the few times I got stuck the support was excellent.
author: Paul S.
pros: Great theme also in general for design. Furthermore, Fantastic support such as with [sensitive content hidden] and colleagues.
cons: I am very happy with it so I can't write something I don't like as the price I am happy with, the support is great, and the features are great.

description: Great! Coming from Elementor, you can see that there are companies that do care about customer service.
author: Arjan S.
pros: I used Elementor in the past, was happy with it. But their support wasnt good, mainly telling you that you have to purcahse plugins. DIVI support is from a different world. Great support, to the point, and a great willingness to make a better website.
cons: Since I have to answer, maybe it's the additional plugins that are relatively expensive. But with their support, luckily not needed.

review_title: Divi 5 is a Total Game-Changer for Speed and Simplicity
description: Whether you are a seasoned developer looking to speed up your workflow or a complete beginner wanting a site that doesn't look "DIY," Divi 5 hits the sweet spot. It balances power and simplicity better than any other builder on the market.
author: Nils S.
pros: Insane Performance: The transition to a new core means the interface is lightning-fast. No more "loading lag"—the builder responds instantly to every click and drag. Their customer support is impressively fast and genuinely helpful. If you ever hit a snag, you aren't left digging through forums—their team is there to get you back on track immediately. Professional Results in Minutes: Between the massive library of pre-made layouts and the intuitive global styles, you can go from a blank page to a high-end corporate site in a fraction of the time it used to take.
cons: After a very short adjustment period, you’ll find yourself navigating the interface like a pro and launching pages in record time.

description: Been using Divi for 4 years. I come from a graphic design background. Divi has allowed me to develop sites for my clients in-house.
author: Bill M.
pros: The evolution from Divi4 to Divi5 is a considerable change but has been well-conceived. It has added a lot of nice capabilities. I have enjoyed the regular notifications and videos on the different capabilities.
cons: There appears to be more upfront prep (set up) work needed before starting a website. Some of this seems like it could be overlapping requirements... but it could be my limited experience with Divi5.

author: Héctor R.
pros: Great Divi customer support! They solved my footer issue quickly. Efficient and helpful assistance!!
cons: Reliable and consistent. No issues to report because the service works very well. Highly recommend..

description: Excellent if I was to stick to premade layouts
author: Natalie M.
pros: Best plugin for Wordpress. It was easy to understand how it works, the learning curve is not as steep compared to other plugins
cons: Still time consuming to customize components as it is not very visual
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Hands down the best drag and drop website theme out there
description: We have absolutely loved it. We used to use Elementor, which isn't bad, but Divi is noticeably better.
author: Amanda V.
pros: The drag and drop is very user friendly, their tutorials are perfect, the support has been great though we have almost never needed it. They regular update the theme and provide a huge library of templates for those who might need them.
cons: Honestly, there isn't anything about Divi that I can complain about. The only downside is that it can sometimes become so large that plugins might occasionally conflict with it but they remedy it quickly every time.
author_profession: Administrator, IT, Tech Support, Customer Service

review_title: An 'Old' Fan of Divi – Still Going Strong in My 70s
description: I've been using Divi for years through a lifetime deal, and it remains one of the best decisions I’ve made for my personal and business websites. Even though I'm in my 70s and not particularly tech-savvy, I find Divi incredibly intuitive. I taught myself HTML and CSS years ago, but Divi takes things to a whole new level—no coding required unless I want to. Overall, I love Divi and recommend it to anyone who wants professional-looking websites without the headaches. It’s empowering to be able to build and maintain my own sites—no matter my age!
author: Wiggie E.
pros: • The visual builder is easy to understand and use, even for non-developers. • Great flexibility in design without needing to touch code. • Excellent customer support—quick, helpful, and professional whenever I’ve needed them. • One-time payment has provided years of value.
cons: • Like any powerful tool, there’s a bit of a learning curve at first. But once you get into it, it flows smoothly.

description: I'm looking forward to the official release of Divi 5 coming up soon. It's smoking fast and I love the whole idea of the option group presets.
author: Cheri D.
pros: Excellent support! They give clear instructions and show video walkthroughs, which helps me learn faster and fix problems more easily.
cons: I've had issues with bugs from the very beginning with earlier iterations of Divi, though, this was partly due to my lack of coding experience. The new Divi 5 Alpha, while buggy, is a game changer for me. When it's working, it works beautifully!

author: Romain G.
pros: Quick and efficient help, as usual :) Support answers are very fast ! I am always very satisfied with Divi support and the quality of the responses provided.

description: great allways great, much better pricing than the competation
author: Saef R.
pros: Easy to use, fast supprt and allways updating
cons: just waitnig for Divi 5 to launche soon inshAllah

description: As a novice, I could not have built my site without Divi and their support staff. Highly recommendable.
author: MATTHEW D.
pros: Ease of use, especially for a bunny like me. I used WordPress about 10 years ago- using Divi now does not compare to those days of frustration. Their support is unequalled- I usually get a response within 10-20 minutes and it's always the correct advice. Can't be said about support from certain plug ins I'm using...
cons: Nothing really! Once you understand the difference between Sections, Rows and Modules, you can't really go badly wrong...!
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Effortless and efficient. Great support team that's always ready to assist.
author: Monique B.
pros: Easy and interactive with great support.
cons: Buggy moments that cause delays when building

description: Really positive. It's fairly straightforward
author: Elle D.
pros: Some of my clients are already comfortable with Divi - making it an obvious choice when setting those websites up.
cons: No cons really... although some other software providers are a little simpler

author: Dilak S.
pros: Divi is a remarkable tool for creating landing pages and developing websites through drag and drop functionality.
cons: I use Divi on a rare cases but I do not face issues at all on my end anyways.

author: Tine L.
pros: It is easy to use and the support is fantasic
cons: Not sure as I like Divi.

description: EXCELLENT BRAVO! Quality of the websites I am creating as a freelancer drastically increased with DIVI.
author: Kirill K.
pros: Super flexible and customizable theme - best customer support ever.
cons: It's very complex sometimes - small things take time. But it's not bad at all.
author_profession: One Man Army - Freelancer - Web Agency

description: Using Divi on my website was a great decision, works great with all the plugins I use. Support is 5* and has quick replies.
author: Lee W.
pros: I choose Divi because it a lightweight theme that does not affect website speed. It has an integrated builder which can save you some costs with different builder plugins. Other plugins like WooCommerce and Rank Math work very well on Divi.
cons: I did not encounter any problems with Divi.

review_title: a very good product, for the value it offers
description: Even if it does not have a free trial version, the product is worth all the money. do what he says. it is very intuitive and easy to use, it is easy to integrate with plugins. I use it to build my wedding photography website, and I am very satisfied .
author: PENISOARA C.
pros: Divi is responsive by design Manage your entire website design using global elements It provides complete design control, all visual and intuitive
cons: the integration with some plugins has problems sometimes It does not have a free version

description: Gaining profits with Divi is the most fantastic experience ever to me.
author: Naomi B.
pros: As someone with limited coding knowledge, using a website-building platform was a game-changer for me. It offered templates, drag-and-drop tools, and customization options, allowing me to create my own website effortlessly. With this platform, I was able to establish my online presence, showcase my work, and reach a wider audience without the hassle or expense of hiring a professional web developer. It's been an invaluable tool for my business growth."
cons: It has never had issues with me or since I started using it.
